items.memory: My parent Thaeion fell ill this season.
I trained this season and failed.
This spring, I felt the heat settle in in Aeanoran.
I spent the season traveling the roads.
The moon this season looked ominous — I prayed for guidance.
The rains this season was late — it was a welcome change.
A wedding was held this season — I could not go and regret it.
This season, I broke a promise.
I wonder what the old gods will bring.
It is written in the runes that the spring is for war.
This spring, I find myself thinking about the road ahead.
I remember this season last year — I was a different person.
The spring cold makes me feel determined.
This season feels fleeting — I cherish it.
I have been wandering more than usual this season — it means something.
I will attend the council next season.
I want to befriend a neighboring tribe.
I plan to write my memoirs.
I should visit Thaeion and honor my parent.
I must prepare for the coming caravan.
I plan to travel to Aeanoran and seek my fortune there.
I am concerned about the strange dreams I have been having — they feel like warnings.
I worry about the river drying up — it has never done that before.
I fear that change is coming — and I am not ready for it.
I worry about the unrest in the mountain — the deep folk are troubled.
